skill.name: frontend-responsive
skill.description: Guides assistants to build mobile-first responsive frontend interfaces using breakpoints, fluid layouts, relative units, accessible tap targets, and cross-device testing.
skill.summary: Apply responsive frontend design practices to React, CSS, and Tailwind interfaces.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static command and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, file extensions, Tailwind breakpoint labels, and ordinary design text. The path traversal finding is valid because SKILL.md references ../../../agent-os/standards/frontend/responsive.md, which points outside the packaged skill and can cause assistants to load unreviewed local content.

medium_findings.title: Path Traversal Reference to Unpackaged Content
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md links to ../../../agent-os/standards/frontend/responsive.md. This relative path escapes the skill directory and can cause the assistant to rely on local content that is not part of the reviewed marketplace package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is directly present in the skill file, and the reported file structure only contains SKILL.md. The risk is dependency on unreviewed local content, not confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Treated as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The line contains Markdown inline code for React file extensions, .tsx and .jsx. There is no shell command, script block, command substitution, or executable instruction on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched backticks only format file extensions in documentation. No evidence found of Ruby, shell execution, or user-controlled command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Patterns in Design Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary words and Tailwind breakpoint notation in descriptive Markdown. No hashing, encryption, credential handling, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a 29-line Markdown guidance document with no code performing cryptography. The matched terms appear in responsive-design prose and Tailwind breakpoint labels.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance in Responsive Design Language
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts match references to device sizes, Claude Code, and testing across breakpoints. These are normal frontend design concepts and do not collect host, network, or system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are used in UI layout guidance. No evidence found of commands or instructions that enumerate operating system, network, account, or hardware details.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path Traversal in Skill Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs readers to refer to a file through a ../../../ path. Marketplace publication should bundle needed guidance or replace the link with reviewed inline instructions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is concrete and semantically relevant because it crosses the skill boundary. It does not show data exfiltration or malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Responsive UI work often fails when layouts are built only for one screen size. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use mobile-first layouts, breakpoints, relative units, and device-aware testing.
actual_capabilities: Guides mobile-first responsive layout decisions for React components.
Recommends CSS Grid, Flexbox, and Tailwind breakpoint utilities.
Promotes relative sizing units for typography, spacing, and containers.
Highlights touch-friendly controls for mobile and tablet interfaces.
Encourages testing across mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints.
limitations: The skill contains guidance only and does not run tests by itself.
It references an external relative file that is not included in the package.
It does not provide complete design tokens or a component library.
It does not define exact project-specific breakpoints beyond common Tailwind labels.

use_cases.title: Make React Screens Responsive
use_cases.description: Update a component so navigation, grids, text, and spacing adapt cleanly from phone to desktop widths.

use_cases.title: Review Mobile Layout Risks
use_cases.description: Ask the assistant to inspect a UI change for fixed-width elements, cramped tap targets, and breakpoint gaps.

use_cases.title: Apply Tailwind Breakpoints
use_cases.description: Create responsive Tailwind class patterns for containers, columns, typography, and spacing.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Component Mobile Friendly
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-responsive skill to update this component for mobile, tablet, and desktop screens. Focus on layout, spacing, typography, and tap targets.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Fixed Layouts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-responsive skill to replace fixed widths and pixel-heavy spacing with fluid containers, relative units, and breakpoint-specific layout behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-responsive skill to review this UI change. Identify responsive layout risks, accessibility concerns for touch users, and missing breakpoint coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Responsive System Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-responsive skill to design a reusable responsive layout pattern for this feature. Include mobile-first behavior, breakpoint transitions, container rules, and testing notes.

output_examples.input: Make a dashboard card grid work on phones and desktops.
output: A mobile-first card stack that becomes a multi-column grid at larger breakpoints.
Relative spacing and typography that scale without creating horizontal overflow.
Testing notes for narrow phones, tablets, and wide desktop screens.

output_examples.input: Review this navigation menu for responsive behavior.
output: Findings about breakpoint coverage, touch target size, and menu wrapping.
Recommended changes for compact screens and desktop navigation.
A short verification checklist for viewport testing.

output_examples.input: Refactor fixed-width CSS in a React layout.
output: A revised layout plan using fluid containers and flexible grid tracks.
Specific guidance for replacing fixed pixels with relative units.
Notes about content wrapping and image behavior across breakpoints.

best_practices: Start with the smallest useful viewport, then add larger breakpoint behavior.
Use relative units and fluid containers before adding breakpoint overrides.
Test interactive elements with realistic touch target sizes and content lengths.
anti_patterns: Building the desktop layout first and patching mobile behavior later.
Using fixed widths that create horizontal scrolling on narrow screens.
Adding breakpoints without testing real content, long labels, and touch interactions.

faq.question: Does this skill write React code?
faq.answer: It guides an assistant while writing or reviewing React components, but it is not a standalone code generator.

faq.question: Can it work with Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill specifically mentions Tailwind responsive utilities such as sm, md, lg, xl, and 2xl.

faq.question: Does it include automated viewport tests?
faq.answer: No. It encourages breakpoint testing, but the package does not include test scripts or tooling.

faq.question: Is it only for mobile layouts?
faq.answer: No. It covers mobile, tablet, and desktop behavior using mobile-first responsive methods.

faq.question: What is the main publication concern?
faq.answer: The skill references a relative path outside its package. That external content should be bundled or removed before publication.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
